topic: Ruff - the Astral Python linter & formatter

questions.q: What is Ruff, what language is it implemented in, and which company maintains it?
questions.gold: Ruff is an extremely fast Python linter and code formatter, implemented in Rust. It is maintained by Astral (the company behind the uv package manager).

questions.q: Which standalone Python tools does Ruff aim to replace/consolidate?
questions.gold: Flake8 (plus dozens of plugins), Black, isort, pydocstyle, pyupgrade, and autoflake.

questions.q: What is Ruff's default value for lint.select (the rule codes enabled by default)?
questions.gold: select = ["E4", "E7", "E9", "F"] - i.e. Pyflakes (F) plus a subset of pycodestyle (E) rules.

questions.q: What are Ruff's default line-length and indent-width values?
questions.gold: line-length defaults to 88; indent-width defaults to 4.

questions.q: In the lint config, what is the difference between `select`/`ignore` and `extend-select`/`extend-ignore`?
questions.gold: select enables specific rules and ignore disables them; extend-select adds rules to an inherited/existing set and extend-ignore removes (ignores) rules from an inherited set, rather than replacing it.

questions.q: What are Ruff's three config file formats, and what is their precedence when multiple exist in the same directory?
questions.gold: pyproject.toml (under [tool.ruff]), ruff.toml, and .ruff.toml. Precedence in the same directory: .ruff.toml > ruff.toml > pyproject.toml.

questions.q: By default, does `ruff check --fix` apply unsafe fixes, and how do you enable unsafe fixes?
questions.gold: No - by default only 'safe' fixes are applied. Unsafe fixes are enabled with the --unsafe-fixes CLI flag (e.g. `ruff check --fix --unsafe-fixes`) or `unsafe-fixes = true` in config.

questions.q: For the formatter, what are the default quote-style and indent-style, and how close is Ruff to Black on real-world code?
questions.gold: Default quote-style is "double" and indent-style is "space". Ruff is a drop-in Black replacement, formatting >99.9% of lines identically on large Black-formatted projects (e.g. Django, Zulip).

questions.q: How do you enable docstring code formatting in Ruff, and what does docstring-code-line-length default to?
questions.gold: Set format.docstring-code-format = true (it defaults to false). docstring-code-line-length defaults to "dynamic", which respects the surrounding code's line-length limit.

questions.q: What source plugin do each of these Ruff rule prefixes come from: F, E/W, I, UP, B, SIM, S, and RUF?
questions.gold: F = Pyflakes; E and W = pycodestyle; I = isort; UP = pyupgrade; B = flake8-bugbear; SIM = flake8-simplify; S = flake8-bandit; RUF = Ruff-specific.

questions.q: How does Ruff's versioning policy work - does it follow standard SemVer, and what do minor vs patch version bumps mean?
questions.gold: It uses a custom (non-SemVer) scheme: the minor version is bumped for breaking changes and the patch version for bug fixes. While pre-1.0 (0.x) the API is unstable; standard SemVer would apply once a stable API is reached.

questions.q: How is a preview-only lint rule (say HYP001) activated, and does selecting it explicitly or via `select = ["ALL"]` enable it without preview mode?
questions.gold: No. Preview rules require preview mode to be on (--preview or preview = true). Without preview mode they are NOT enabled even by extend-select = ["HYP001"], extend-select = ["HYP"], or select = ["ALL"]; preview mode must be enabled alongside the selection.

questions.q: What command runs Ruff's built-in language server, in which Ruff versions did it reach beta and stable, and what is the official VS Code extension ID?
questions.gold: Run it with `ruff server` (the native Rust LSP, a replacement for ruff-lsp). It was beta in Ruff v0.4.5 and stabilized in v0.5.3. The official VS Code extension ID is charliermarsh.ruff.

questions.q: According to these docs, is Ruff a type checker that can replace mypy/pyright?
questions.gold: No. The FAQ explicitly states 'Ruff is a linter, not a type checker.' It does not replace a type checker; type checkers (Mypy, Pyright, Pyre) catch type errors Ruff misses and are complementary. (Astral's separate type checker is ty, not Ruff.)
